attacking_style: Does not typically initiate attacks; wins from reduced sprints after selective climbing or hard team pace. Rare breakaway attempts occur early and are caught before finale.
team_tactics: INEOS delivers him via sustained tempo riding (not leadout trains) to drop pure sprinters while preserving his kick. Kwiatkowski and Sheffield provide final positioning support when available.
terrain_preference: Hilly sprints and summit finishes after moderate climbing suit best. Exposed on longest flat stages and steep sustained climbs where 'pedaling squares' observed.
vulnerability: Psychological fragility in solo situations (looks back repeatedly under pressure). Stage race fatigue accumulates visibly. Crashes frequently at critical race moments (3 documented incidents).
key_patterns: Thrives in reduced sprints after climbs eliminate pure sprinters; multiple podiums in this scenario.
Team dedicates full resources but execution falters in flat sprint stages versus specialists.
Crashes or mechanicals at decisive moments repeatedly compromise results (4 separate incidents documented).
Stage race fatigue manifests visibly: 'pedaling squares' and time losses after sustained racing efforts.
Psychological uncertainty when isolated: looked back repeatedly despite being solo and strong enough.
White jersey defense successful but GC threat unclear; maintains position without testing rivals upward.
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 observations show DNF mechanicals, crashes, team execution failures, and caught-from-breakaway pattern suggesting reduced competitive edge versus 2022 hilly sprint wins
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 4
